disease.name: Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus
disease.note: Type 1 DM results from autoimmune destruction of pancreatic beta cells, leading to absolute insulin deficiency. Peak onset in childhood and adolescence. Lifelong insulin dependence is managed with basal-bolus regimens (MDI) or continuous subcutaneous insulin infusion (CSII). Carbohydrate counting, CGM, and automated insulin delivery systems improve outcomes. DKA remains a life-threatening acute complication.

severityAssessment.severityLabel: If DKA with AMS or severe hypo -> EMERGENCY; if glucose >400 or recurrent DKA -> SEVERE; if HbA1c <8% stable -> MODERATE

subtypes.name: Multiple Daily Injections (MDI) Basal-Bolus Regimen
subtypes.management: Total daily insulin (TDI) ~0.5-0.6 U/kg initially. Basal 40-50% TDI as glargine U100 (0.2-0.3 U/kg) once daily. Bolus 50-60% divided across meals. Insulin-to-carb ratio: 1U per 10-15g carbs. Correction factor: 1U lowers glucose ~50 mg/dL (1800/TDI rule). Titrate based on CGM and pre-meal glucose trends.

subtypes.name: Continuous Subcutaneous Insulin Infusion (CSII / Insulin Pump)
subtypes.management: Pump basal rate ~40-50% of TDI delivered over 24h with multiple hourly rates. Meal boluses via pump bolus calculator using ICR and CF. Hybrid closed-loop systems (MiniMed 780G, Tandem Control-IQ, Omnipod 5) automate basal with CGM. Change infusion set q2-3 days. Sick day rules: never disconnect >2h, check ketones if glucose >250.

subtypes.name: Diabetic Ketoacidosis (DKA) Management Protocol
subtypes.management: IV fluids: 0.9% NS 10-20mL/kg over 1h, then 250-500mL/h. Insulin: regular IV 0.1 U/kg bolus, then 0.1 U/kg/h. When glucose <250, switch to D5 0.45 NS and decrease insulin to 0.05 U/kg/h. Potassium: start when K <5.3, goal 4-5.5. Bicarbonate only if pH <6.9. Monitor: glucose q1h, BMP q2-4h, VBG q2-4h. Transition to SC insulin when eating and pH >7.3.

bedside.name: Capillary blood glucose (CBG) monitoring
bedside.indication: all patients, before meals and at bedtime

laboratory.name: C-peptide (fasting or stimulated)
laboratory.indication: differentiate T1 from T2 at diagnosis

laboratory.name: Islet autoantibody panel (GAD-65, IA-2, insulin, ZnT8)
Basic metabolic panel (glucose, Na, K, Cl, CO2, BUN, Cr)

management.protocolReference: ADA Standards of Care 2024, ISPAD Clinical Practice Guidelines, Joint British Diabetes Societies DKA Guidelines

pathways.treatment: Hospital admission if DKA or significant hyperglycemia. Insulin initiation: TDI 0.5 U/kg/day. Basal-bolus education. Carbohydrate counting training. CGM initiation. Diabetes educator consult. Discharge with follow-up in 1-2 weeks. School/director care plan.

IV fluids 0.9% NS 10mL/kg. Insulin IV 0.1U/kg bolus then 0.1U/kg/h. Replace K when <5.3. Monitor glucose q1h, electrolytes q2h. Transition to SC insulin when pH >7.3 and patient eating. Give SC basal 2h before stopping IV insulin.

pathways.criteria: glucose <70 mg/dL with or without symptoms
pathways.treatment: If conscious: 15g fast-acting carbs (glucose tabs 3-4, juice 4oz, glucose gel). Recheck in 15min. Repeat if still <70. Severe (unconscious/seizure): glucagon IM/SC 0.5mg (<5y) or 1mg (>=5y) or nasal glucagon 3mg. IV dextrose 50% 25g if IV access. Once recovered, give complex carb + protein.
